title: Security Roles and Responsibilities Assignment Completeness

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=hr sourcetype="iam:pci_role_assignment" earliest=-1d
| where match(role_name,"(?i)CISO|PCI\\s*Lead|IR\\s*Lead|NetSec|DBA\\s*CHD)")
| eval missing_primary=isnull(primary_user) OR primary_user=""
| eval missing_backup=isnull(backup_user) OR backup_user=""
| where missing_primary=1 OR missing_backup=1
| table role_name, primary_user, backup_user, effective_date
| sort role_name
value: Verifies each PCI-defined security role in the responsibility matrix has a named primary and backup assignee in the HR or IAM feed so accountability for security activities is not ambiguous during audits.
implementation: (1) Align `role_name` strings with PCI responsibility matrix. (2) Daily sync from Workday/Azure AD groups. (3) Page HRIS on gaps. (4) Quarterly executive sign-off stored in GRC.
visualization: Table (gaps), matrix visual (role x assignee), single value (open gaps).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
